_evasion.description: Fawkes supports domain fronting — connecting to a CDN endpoint (e.g., Cloudflare, Azure CDN) that forwards traffic to the actual C2 server. The network connection appears to go to a legitimate domain.
_evasion.bypass_attempt: Using CDN domain fronting to hide C2 destination behind legitimate-looking domain
_evasion.notes: KNOWN GAP. Domain fronting makes the destination.ip and destination.fqdn appear legitimate (e.g., a CDN IP). Our current T1071.001 rule uses connection frequency patterns that may miss fronted traffic if timing looks like normal web browsing. Mitigation: TLS certificate inspection (SNI vs Host header mismatch), JA3/JA3S fingerprinting.
_evasion.remediation: Add network-level controls: TLS inspection for SNI/Host mismatch, proxy logs showing destination after fronting resolution
